methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

data.narrative:
Sygaldry Technologies is a Series A–stage AI & Software company founded by Chad Rigetti, Idalia Friedson, and Michael Keiser, which has raised $139M to date. The relevant comp universe comprises 9 private peers and 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with direct stage-and-sector comparables including Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, and Together AI.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($139M) as a proxy base value and applies a typical Series A step-up multiplier of 2.0x, yielding $278M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-stage, same-sector peers: Reflection AI ($25.0B), Applied Intuition ($15.0B), and Together AI ($8.3B), producing a median valuation of $15.0B with a range spanning $8.3B to $25.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $236M (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $7.6B. The substantial spread reflects material methodological divergence: stage-progression conservatively extrapolates from deployment of capital, while peer-median reflects market pricing of similar-stage AI companies at significantly higher multiples. Key uncertainties include whether Sygaldry commands comparable unit economics and growth trajectory to the peer set, and whether the peer valuations reflect durable market consensus or sector-specific inflation.
